title: Dawnn vignette
text: Installation instructions given GitHub README. vignette, assume Tensorflow installed conda environment called tf_env, necessarily environment contains Dawnn. docker image run vignette available DockerHub georgehallucl/dawnn_benchmarking.

title: Dawnn vignette
text: Following installation Dawnn package, Dawnn model, Tensorflow Python package, now ready run tool. GitHub README, assume following simple example cells Seurat dataset >1000 cells, PCA reduction, meta.data slot condition_name contains name condition cell belongs (either Condition1 Condition2). wish associate label Condition1 positive log-fold changes results (Condition2 thus associated negative).
code: library(dawnn) library(Seurat) cells <- run_dawnn(cells, label_names = "label", label_pos_lfc = "Condition1",                    reduced_dim = "pca", tf_conda_env = "tf_env", verbosity = 0)

title: Dawnn vignette
text: run_dawnn four required parameters: outline optional parameters later vignette.

title: Dawnn vignette
text: Dawnn’s outputs stored meta.data slots cells. outputs : first two outputs likely useful. dawnn_[lda/gda]_verdict tells us whether Dawnn called cell region [local/global] differential abundance dawnn_lfc contains estimated log2-fold change abundance label_pos_lfc (relative label) neighbourhood cell. second quantity independent whether user searching local global DA. Let’s first plot labels cell identify manually cluster exhibit differential abundance.  , appears cluster bottom UMAP roughly even split two conditions, whilst two clusters exhibit differential abundance towards one . see whether Dawnn detected colouring UMAP according Dawnn’s verdict local differential abundance (dawnn_lda_verdict).  expected, Dawnn detects differential abundance bottom cluster two. want investigate estimated log2-fold change abundance Condition1 compared Condition2, can colour cells according dawnn_lfc.  expected, two clusters identified generally exhibiting local differential abundance estimated log2-fold changes far 0, whereas cluster bottom UMAP quantity close 0. dawnn_scores contains direct output Dawnn’s neural network, .e. estimated probability cell drawn sample associated label_pos_lfc (case, Condition1). dawnn_scores converted dawnn_lfc log2(cells$dawnn_scores / (1 - cells$dawnn_scores)).  Finally, dawnn_p_vals_[lda|gda] contains, cell, p-value associated testing null hypothesis “cell region [local|global] differential abundance”. p-values used determine calls made dawnn_[lda|gda]_verdict. plot cell’s p-value test whether region local differential abundance.
code: DimPlot(cells, group.by = "label", pt.size = 0.1,         cols = c("#FFC107", "#D81B60")) DimPlot(cells, group.by = "dawnn_lda_verdict", pt.size = 0.1,         cols = c("#FFC107", "#D81B60")) FeaturePlot(cells, "dawnn_lfc") + viridis::scale_color_viridis(option = "cividis") FeaturePlot(cells, "dawnn_scores") + viridis::scale_color_viridis(option = "cividis") FeaturePlot(cells, "dawnn_p_vals_lda") + viridis::scale_color_viridis(option = "cividis")

title: Dawnn vignette
text: main function run_dawnn number optional parameters: options can set follow:
code: cells <- run_dawnn(cells, label_names = "labels", label_pos_lfc = "Condition1",                    reduced_dim = "pca", tf_conda_env = "tf_env", n_dims = 20,                    nn_model = "~/another_dawnn_model.h5",                    recalculate_graph = FALSE, alpha = 0.05, verbosity = 0,                    seed = 42)

title: Dawnn vignette
text: can use download_model’s model_file_path parameter change location model downloaded. non-default location must passed run_dawnn using nn_model parameter.
code: download_model(..., model_file_path = "~/Documents/new_model_location.h5") run_dawnn(..., nn_model = "~/Documents/new_model_location.h5")

what: Downloading a model from another location
title: Dawnn vignette
text: neural network model Dawnn can downloaded non-default url using model_url parameter. might useful trained model different K, instance
code: download_model(..., model_url = "example.com/another_model_url.h5")

title: Authors and Citation
text: George Hall. Author, maintainer. Sergi Castellano. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hall G, Castellano S (2026). dawnn: Differential Abundance Neural Networks. R package version 2.0.0, https://george-hall-ucl.github.io/dawnn/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {dawnn: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks},   author = {George Hall and Sergi Castellano},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2.0.0},   url = {https://george-hall-ucl.github.io/dawnn/}, }

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks
text: easiest way use Dawnn Docker. avoids needing install R package Python dependencies.

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks
text: Install Docker start Docker Desktop.

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks
text: R, save Seurat dataset .rds object:
code: saveRDS(my_cells, "my_cells.rds")

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks
text: terminal, run Dawnn though Docker: Dawnn’s outputs written dawnn_out.csv.
code: docker run --rm --volume "$(pwd):/tmp/in_mnt" --workdir /tmp/in_mnt \     dawnn_standalone '     run_dawnn(cells = readRDS("my_cells.rds"), label_names = "label",               label_pos_lfc = "Condition1", reduced_dim = "pca",               tf_conda_env = "tf_env", verbosity = 1) ' > dawnn_out.csv

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks
text: Back R, load Dawnn’s outputs Seurat object’s metadata: can now use Dawnn’s outputs measure differential abundance data. See run_dawnn() vignette("dawnn") details parameters outputs.
code: dawnn_out <- read.csv("dawnn_out.csv") my_cells@meta.data <- cbind(my_cells@meta.data,                             dawnn_out[rownames(my_cells@meta.data), ])

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks
text: don’t want use Docker, can install run Dawnn within R. Dawnn package currently available Github. Note need install conda Step 3. Assume cells Seurat dataset PCA reduction, meta.data slot condition_name contains name condition cell belongs (either Condition1 Condition2) want label Condition1 associated positive log-fold change. Dawnn requires least 1,001 cells. assume TensorFlow installed tf_env conda environment. Dawnn parameters listed . details, see run_dawnn() vignette("dawnn").
code: # Step 1: Install Dawnn package (may need to install `remotes` package first) remotes::install_github("george-hall-ucl/dawnn")  # Step 2: Download Dawnn's model # By default, model stored at ~/.dawnn/dawnn_nn_model.h5 dawnn::download_model()  # Step 3: Install Tensorflow in own conda environment conda create -y -n tf_env -c conda-forge python=3.12.4 \     && conda run -n tf_env pip install tensorflow library(Seurat) library(dawnn)  cells <- run_dawnn(cells, label_names = "condition_name",                    label_pos_lfc = "Condition1", reduced_dim = "pca",                    tf_conda_env = "tf_env")

title: Differential Abundance with Neural Networks

what: Identify which cells are in regions of differential abundance using Dawnn. — run_dawnn
title: Identify which cells are in regions of differential abundance using Dawnn. — run_dawnn
text: run_dawnn() main function used run Dawnn. takes Seurat dataset identifies cells regions differential abundance. Dawnn requires least 1,001 cells.

title: Identify which cells are in regions of differential abundance using Dawnn. — run_dawnn
code: run_dawnn(   cells,   label_names,   label_1,   label_2,   reduced_dim,   n_dims = 10,   nn_model = "~/.dawnn/dawnn_nn_model.h5",   recalculate_graph = TRUE,   alpha = 0.1,   verbosity = 2,   seed = 123,   tf_conda_env = NULL )

title: Identify which cells are in regions of differential abundance using Dawnn. — run_dawnn
text: cells Seurat object containing dataset. label_names String containing name meta.data slot `cells' containing labels cell. label_1 String containing name one labels. label_2 String containing name label. reduced_dim String containing name dimensionality reduction use. n_dims Integer number dimensions use computing graph (optional, default 10). nn_model String containing path model's .hdf5 file (optional, default "~/.dawnn/dawnn_nn_model.h5"). recalculate_graph Boolean whether recalculate KNN graph. FALSE, one stored cells object used (optional, default = TRUE). alpha Numeric target false discovery rate supplied Benjamini–Yekutieli procedure (optional, default 0.1, .e. 10%). verbosity Integer much output print. 0: silent; 1: normal output; 2: display messages predict() function. seed Integer random seed (optional, default 123). tf_conda_env Conda environment TensorFlow installed, useful unavailable current environment (optional, default NULL).

title: Identify which cells are in regions of differential abundance using Dawnn. — run_dawnn
text: Seurat dataset `cells' added metadata: dawnn_scores (output Dawnn's model cell); dawnn_lfc (estimated log2-fold change neighbourhood cell); dawnn_p_vals (p-values associated hypothesis tests whether cell region differential abundance; dawnn_da_verdict (Boolean output Dawnn indicating whether considers cell region differential abundance).

title: Identify which cells are in regions of differential abundance using Dawnn. — run_dawnn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ run_dawnn(cells = dataset, label_names = "condition", nn_model = "my_model.h5", reduced_dim = "pca", n_dims = 50, recalculate_graph = FALSE, alpha = 0.2, verbosity = 0, seed = 42, tf_conda_env = "my_tensorflow_env") } # }
